settings.title: Desktop Overlay · Settings
settings.cover: Media cover cache dirs (fallback scan when SMTC has no cover)
settings.cover.tip: Electron-shell players like NetEase Cloud Music don't write the cover into SMTC. Add their local album-cache directory (or any folder holding covers) here. When SMTC has no thumbnail, Lumen picks the most recently written image from these dirs. Known players are also auto-scanned.

prop.fieldHint: Each field can switch between [Value | Formula | Variable]: Formula mode can insert functions; Variable mode references global variables (gv).
prop.interaction.title: Click Action (Triggered by Left-Click in Desktop Mode)
prop.hint.runApp: Argument: Start menu app name, or .lnk / executable path (click '{0}' to choose)
prop.hint.mediaControl: Pick from dropdown: Play / Pause / Next / Previous / Stop
prop.hint.switchPage: Pick from dropdown: Next page / Previous page / specific page number
prop.hint.command: Argument: command line (run with cmd /c; click '{0}' to pick a program then append arguments)
prop.hint.switchPreset: Pick from dropdown: preset name (Day/Night/...) or Previous / Next (cycled across all pages)
prop.hint.default: Fill in the argument after selecting an action type
prop.hint.runFlow: Argument: choose the index of this atom's flow to trigger
prop.hint.setVar: Argument: name|value (value supports $formula$ / $gv(x)$ for read & write; use $gv(x)$ to read a variable)
prop.hint.delay: Argument: delay in milliseconds (ms); the flow waits before continuing
prop.hint.readFile: Argument: file path|variable name[|JSON field]; reads txt/json content into a global variable (JSON field e.g. a.b.c or a[0].b extracts a sub-item)
prop.flow.title: Flow (Automatically Runs an Action Sequence When Conditions Are Met)
prop.flow.desc: The condition is a boolean formula, e.g. bi(level) < 20 or mi(playing) = "Playing"; evaluated only in desktop mode. Once = fires once when it becomes true, While = fires every cycle while true.
prop.flow.condTooltip: Boolean formula, e.g. bi(level) < 20 or mi(playing) = "Playing"
prop.flow.presetCond: Preset Conditions (Select to Fill the Formula Above)
prop.preset.gvTrue: Global Variable Is True (e.g. gv flag)
prop.flow.exec: Execution Flow (Run a Set of Actions in List Order)
prop.formula.tooltip: Enter a formula expression, e.g. bi(level) or if(bi(level)>20, High, Low)
addcomp.title: Add Component
prop.func.title: Insert Function
prop.formula.actionNoPreview: (Action Functions Are Not Executed in Preview)
prop.formula.bound: Formula Bound: This field is driven by a formula
prop.formula.unbound: Not Bound: This field is a static value or variable

gv.title: Global Variables (gv)
gv.desc: Shared across atoms and pages. Reference it in the property editor's "Variable" mode, or read it in a formula via gv(name).
gv.empty: (No variables yet — click "Add Variable" to create one)
color.title: Colour Picker
pagebg.title: Page Settings
pagebg.formulaHint: Enter a formula (no outer $); the result must be a colour. Example: si(cpu)>80 ? "#FFFF3228" : "#FF1E1E1E"
pagebg.sceneDesc: Save the current page (atoms + grid + background) as a reusable scene preset; applying it replaces the whole page.
profile.title: Profiles
profile.hint: Each profile contains: all pages (with widgets) + per-page settings + global variables + user presets.
profile.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete profile "{0}"? This cannot be undone.
profile.importDone: Imported "{0}". You can switch to it from the list.
file.title: Select File
func.df:
Current time formatted in KLWP style.
Common patterns: df(HH:mm) → 14:30 · df(yyyy-MM-dd) → 2026-07-18 · df(hh:mm A) → 02:30 PM
Supports: HH=24h hh=12h mm=min ss=sec yyyy=year MM=month dd=day
func.tf: Convert seconds to readable duration. tf(3661) → 1:01:01 · tf(60) → 1:00 · tf(86400) → 24:00:00. Pair with tu() for time-difference display.
func.ts: Generate or format Unix timestamps (seconds). ts() → 1784369600; ts(yyyy-MM-dd) → 2026-07-18. Useful for displaying custom dates.
func.tu: Current UTC time as string in yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss format. Compare with df() to determine timezone offset.
func.tz: Local timezone offset in hours. China → +8 · Japan → +9 · UK winter → 0
func.si:
Live system metrics via PDH counters.
CPU: cpu(total%) / cpu0~cpuN(per-core%)
Memory: mem / memused / memtotal
Disk: diskfree / disktotal / diskp
Network: netup / netdown (KB/s)
Display: rwidth / rheight / density / dark(dark mode?)
Usage: if(si(cpu)>80, "High Load", "Normal")
func.bi:
Battery status.
bi(level) → 75(battery %) · bi(plugged) → True(charging) / False · bi(charging) → True/False
func.dp: DPI scaling conversion. Converts UI design pixels(dpx) to physical pixels. dp(100) on 150% scaling returns 150.
func.gv:
Read global or component variables.
gv(0,name) → global variable gv(0,accent) reads accent
gv("",name) → local component variable
gv("componentId",name) → cross-component read
gv(0,listVar,2) → 2nd item of a list
Combo: if(gv(0,theme)=="dark", #1E1E1E, #FFFFFF)
func.if:
Conditional: returns 'true' value if condition is true, otherwise 'false' value.
Examples: if(si(cpu)>50, "Busy", "Idle") · if(gv(0,mode)=="C", temp_C, temp_F)
Supports: == != > < >= <= && ||
func.tc:
Text processing with multiple commands.
tc(cut, text, 5) → first 5 chars
tc(ell, long text, 10) → truncate with ellipsis
tc(reg, text, /pattern/replacement) → regex replace
tc(up, text) → UPPERCASE
tc(low, text) → lowercase
tc(cap, text) → Capitalize
func.uc: Convert text to upper case. uc(hello) → HELLO. No effect on CJK characters.
func.re:
Regex substitution. re(abc123, \\d+, #) → abc#
re(Phone: 13800138000, \\d{4}(?=\\d{4}), ****) → Phone: ****3800
func.mi:
Media information via SMTC (System Media Transport Controls).
mi(title) → track title · mi(artist) → artist · mi(album) → album
mi(app) → player name · mi(playing) → True/False · mi(pos) → current position (sec) · mi(dur) → total duration (sec)
mi(cover) → cover image path (for Image atom)
mi(cover, vibrant) → extract dominant color: vibrant/muted/light/dark/dominant
Combo: if(mi(playing), mi(title), "Not Playing")
func.mu:
Math operations dispatcher. mu(op, x[, y, z])
Basic: mu(round, 3.14159, 2) → 3.14 · mu(abs, -5) → 5
Calc: mu(pow, 2, 10) → 1024 · mu(sqrt, 100) → 10
Trig: mu(sin, 30) → 0.5 · mu(cosd, 60) → 0.5
Stats: mu(min, 3, 7, 2) → 2 · mu(max, 3, 7, 2) → 7
Interp: mu(lerp, 0, 100, 0.5) → 50 · mu(clamp, 150, 0, 100) → 100
Dist: mu(dist, 0, 0, 3, 4) → 5
func.ce:
Color operations. ce(op, color[, amount]) → new color
Read: ce(red, #FF3333) → 51 · ce(hue, #39F) → 210
Adjust: ce(lum, #39F, 20) → brighter 20% · ce(alpha, #39F, 128) → 50% transparent
Transform: ce(invert, #39F) → complementary · ce(blend, #39F, #F33, 50) → blend
Use with gv for dynamic theme color adjustments
func.bp:
Image palette extraction (median-cut algorithm). bp(type[, source])
Types: vibrant / muted / light / dark / dominant
Omit source → wallpaper; source=cover → album art
Combo: bp(vibrant, mi(cover)) → current track's primary color
Works with file paths: bp(dominant, C:/photo.jpg)
func.wg:
RSS/XML external data fetch. wg(url[, index, field])
Fields: title / link / desc
No index → item count; no index+field → raw XML
Combo: wg(https://rss.example.com/feed.xml, 1, title) → latest article headline
func.fl:
Select the Nth argument from a list (1-based).
fl(2, Red, Green, Blue) → Green
Pair with gv for dynamic list selection: fl(gv(0,colorIdx), Red, Green, Blue)
func.rng:
Random number generator.
rng() → 0~1 random decimal · rng(1, 6) → 1~6 integer (dice roll)
rng(1000, 9999) → 4-digit random number
atom.hint.animWhen: Leave empty = play automatically with no condition. Enter a boolean formula (e.g. mi(playing)="Playing" or si(cpu)>80): on the rising edge (false→true) the entrance→loop plays; when false it stops and resets.
atom.hint.glyph: Hex code point, e.g. E974 (sync icon)
atom.hint.pathData: Only for SVG Path. Coordinate space 0..100, stretched to atom size. e.g. M10 50 L50 10 L90 50 L50 90 Z
atom.hint.textureColor: Fill affects only Plastic/Metal/Neon. Frosted/Glass/Liquid/Holographic are translucent; Wood/Marble/Carbon/Paper/Fabric use fixed colors.
atom.hint.strokeW: Stroke width. For a line, use a Rectangle with one side very small.
comp.var.empty: (No internal variables yet. Click "Add Variable" to define component parameters)
comp.var.noSchema: (Define parameters under "Internal Variables" first, then override instance values here)
